title: reasonable price for the Theatrical Edition laser disc set
display_title: reasonable price for the Theatrical Edition laser disc set
wikitext:
[]: reasonable price for the Theatrical Edition laser disc set?             Quote Post by sipth » Mon Jul 14, 2014 8:02 am So I have been scouting ebay for awhile now any they are all pretty pricey. I am wondering how much do you guys think is worth it for one that has all the merch inside that is in good condition? And isit even worth it to get? As I've seen people buying it for pretty damn cheap, the last I saw was a guy on reddit purchasing it for $40~

[]: Re: reasonable price for the Theatrical Edition laser disc s             Quote Post by svenge » Sun Jul 20, 2014 9:14 am sipth wrote: So I have been scouting ebay for awhile now any they are all pretty pricey. I am wondering how much do you guys think is worth it for one that has all the merch inside that is in good condition? And isit even worth it to get? As I've seen people buying it for pretty damn cheap, the last I saw was a guy on reddit purchasing it for $40~

[]: Re: reasonable price for the Theatrical Edition laser disc s             Quote Post by sipth » Sun Jul 20, 2014 9:46 am svenge wrote: As a proud owner of said box set, I'd say it's worth it. Between the Laserdiscs themselves (of which there are four in the higher-quality CAV format), the cover art thereof, an 800-page storyboard book, 18x Cardass Masters picture cards, 3x phone cards, an Eva Series UFO catcher plushie, a 5-inch Rei gashopon figure, and an unassembled Eva-01 Bandai LMHG model kit, there's a lot of value there. One of the problems is that most of eBay's Japanese sellers greatly over-inflate their prices, likely due to the vast majority of the US market's buyers being completely unable to do any market research on their own to gauge the actual value of a given item (due to both the language barrier and the fact that the Japanese used market exists mostly on sites that US buyers don't frequent e.g. Yahoo Auctions Japan, Rakuten Japan, etc.). The other problem is the sheer mass of the box set. It's about 5kg / 10lbs (the Laserdiscs themselves are rather heavy, and the other swag in the box doesn't help either) and since most copies of the box set are located in Japan, it has to get to get shipped to the buyer somehow. According to Japan Post's website, it costs ¥5000 to ship 5kg from Japan to Singapore via SAL (~2 weeks), and ¥6000 via EMS (3-4 days). Of course there's always Surface Mail at ¥3300, as long as one can wait 2 months for delivery... As for the price you should target, it's hard to say as they don't come up for sale too often outside of the Japanese-language marketplaces. I got exceedingly lucky a couple years ago to buy it from a seller located in the US for only US$40 + $17 shipping (as domestic mail in the US is relatively inexpensive) but obviously that's something that doesn't reoccur often. That said, I'd look for a before-shipping price in the $75-$125 range (in USD) depending on condition and completeness. /end "Antiques Roadshow" mode

[]: Re: reasonable price for the Theatrical Edition laser disc s             Quote Post by svenge » Sun Jul 20, 2014 3:25 pm sipth wrote: Ahh! This must be your website! http://svenge.pcriot.com/ . Thanks to the information on what's in the box set from your website I made my purchase not long ago! I'm really excited for it to arrive, the thing is that I dont know how long it will take as the seller is Japanese it's kind of hard to communicate, all he said was that it is registered and is unsure if there is a tracking number a not . According to the Ebay seller I bought it from, it has everything intact and it's in pretty good condition. I'm actually happy that you are pleased with your box set as this is my 2nd anime purchase, The first bring a small figma! I got mine for $60 USD and the shipping was........ 60 USD.

[]: Re: reasonable price for the Theatrical Edition laser disc s             Quote Post by sipth » Tue Jul 22, 2014 10:17 am svenge wrote: I'm sure you'll enjoy it quite a bit whenever it arrives. Be advised though that the video playback quality of LDs greatly varies depending on the particular model of player you're using (due to the fact that LD video is actually analog )...

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Jul 22, 2014 8:57 pm Congratulations! Once you buy something Eva, it can be hard to stop. Got the gashapon, Eva-01 kit, and phone cards off Akadot Retail years ago. They took a box set and broke up the contents. Gashapon and kit are unassembled as I'm one of those people who normally keeps figures and toys MIP.

[]:             Quote Post by sipth » Wed Jul 23, 2014 2:29 am AR-99 wrote: Congratulations! Once you buy something Eva, it can be hard to stop. Got the gashapon, Eva-01 kit, and phone cards off Akadot Retail years ago. They took a box set and broke up the contents.
